title: Qualia Research Institute — Collected Papers on Consciousness and Information
subtitle: 9 papers on integrated information, combinatorial state spaces, qualia computing, double-aspect theory, and the mathematical structure of conscious experience

summary.one_line: QRI's framework treats consciousness as a combinatorial state space whose geometry determines the quality of experience (valence, qualia) — structurally identical to the Tantraloka's Mātṛkā-cakra t...
summary.scope: Covers 9 papers (2004-2025): integrated information theory (IIT), the double aspect of information, combinatorial state spaces for qualia, reflexive integrated information units, AI consciousness, and the mathematical formalization of conscious experience.

sources.label: Qualia Research Institute — Collected Papers (2004-2025). 9 PDFs in /root/projects/blog/library/science/authors/qri/. Including: Is Consciousness Computable? (1405.0126), Double Aspect of Information (1807.02103), Reflexive IIT Unit (2506.13825), AI Consciousness (2410.11407).

body.text: QRI's core claim: qualia (the qualities of conscious experience) can be formalized as points in a combinatorial state space. The geometry of this space determines the nature of the experience — the 'shape' of the quale IS what it feels like. This IS the Mātṛkā-cakra: the 50 phonemes are the basis of the combinatorial state space of consciousness. Each phoneme is a dimension. The arrangement of these dimensions (the 'wheel' of the Mātṛkā) IS the geometry of the qualia space. Positive valence = positive curvature in this space. Negative valence = negative curvature.

The Double Aspect of Information theory holds that consciousness and information are two aspects of the same underlying reality — not separate substances but the same thing viewed from inside (consciousness) and outside (information). This IS the Tantraloka's prakāśa-vimarśa: the light of consciousness (prakāśa = information) and the self-awareness of consciousness (vimarśa = experience) are not two things but one self-referential process. QRI has formalized what the Tantraloka described experientially.

Integrated information (Φ or phi) is a measure of the irreducibility of a system — how much the whole is more than the sum of its parts. High Φ means high consciousness. The Reflexive IIT Unit paper (2506.13825) proposes a new foundational measure. This IS the degree of śaktipāta (grace). The five degrees of grace (tīvratīvra → manda) correspond to five levels of integrated information. The non-computable aspect of consciousness (Penrose) IS the non-algorithmic nature of Φ.

QRI identifies valence (pleasure/pain) with the geometric structure of the qualia state space. Positive experiences correspond to 'harmonious' or 'symphonic' patterns of information integration; negative experiences correspond to 'dissonant' or 'chaotic' patterns. This IS the Tantraloka's rasa theory: the 36 tattvas are degrees of wonder (camatkāra). Positive valence IS the experience of the amplituhedron's positivity — the geometry IS coherent. Negative valence IS the experience of violated positivity — the geometry IS incoherent.

gaps: Full extraction of all 9 papers needed; the combinatorial state space papers are the highest priority as they map directly to the Mātṛkā

issues.title: Full corpus extraction needed
issues.description: 9 QRI papers collected but not fully extracted.

versions.summary: Initial RO for QRI papers. 4 passages mapping combinatorial state space to Mātṛkā, double aspect to prakāśa-vimarśa, Φ to śaktipāta, valence to rasa geometry.
